ads.title: Brand introduction image
ads.primary_text:
Most people who come to our workshops already have a financial plan. They come to find out if it's coordinated.

On Wednesday, May 14, wealth manager Brandon Beck and estate planning attorney Ehren Frey are hosting a complimentary lunch at The Capital Grille in Naples. The focus: the gaps that show up between your investment plan, your estate documents, and your tax strategy when nobody's looking at them together.

Bring your spouse. This one works better as a couple.

24 seats. Complimentary lunch. Couples welcome.

RSVP here: [Landing Page Link]

ads.title: Educational carousel (5 cards)
ads.primary_text:
You've done the planning. But when was the last time your estate attorney, your tax advisor, and your wealth manager were in the same conversation?

On Wednesday, May 14 at The Capital Grille in Naples, Brandon Beck and estate planning attorney Ehren Frey are sitting down together to walk through the four areas where plans tend to fall out of sync. Complimentary lunch included.

24 seats. Couples welcome.

RSVP here: [Landing Page Link]

*This presentation is for educational purposes only and is not legal, tax, or investment advice. The guest attorney is not affiliated with Confluence Financial Partners. Please consult your own advisors regarding your individual situation.
ads.headline: Where Plans Fall Out of Sync
ads.hook: You've done the planning. But when was the last time all your advisors were in the same conversation?

cards.label: Hook
cards.caption: You have an investment advisor. You have estate documents. You probably have a tax strategy. The question is whether they're working together — or just sitting in different folders.

cards.label: Control & Autonomy
cards.caption: If something happened tomorrow, does your family know who steps in? Do your documents reflect what you want them to say — or what you wanted five years ago?

cards.label: Roth Conversions
cards.caption: The window for Roth conversions isn't open forever — and the right timing depends on your full tax picture. It's one of the topics we'll walk through together.

cards.label: Legacy
cards.caption: Your heirs shouldn't have to guess your intentions. We'll talk about putting wishes in writing and how charitable giving fits the bigger picture.

cards.label: CTA
cards.caption:
Wednesday, May 14 at 12:00 PM
The Capital Grille, Naples
Complimentary lunch. 24 seats. Couples welcome.
Reserve yours.

ads.title: Infographic / visual concept
ads.primary_text:
There's a real difference between having a financial plan and having a coordinated one.

With coordination: your estate documents name the people you trust, your Roth conversions are timed with your full tax picture in mind, and your heirs aren't left guessing.

Without it: a court may choose who manages your affairs. Tax timing works against you on distributions. Your family sorts it out under pressure.

On Wednesday, May 14, Brandon Beck and estate planning attorney Ehren Frey are hosting a complimentary lunch at The Capital Grille in Naples to explore what a coordinated plan looks like and the questions worth asking together.

When: Wednesday, May 14 at 12:00 PM
Where: The Capital Grille, Naples

Couples welcome.

RSVP here: [Landing Page Link]

*This presentation is for educational purposes only and is not legal, tax, or investment advice. The guest attorney is not affiliated with Confluence Financial Partners. Please consult your own advisors regarding your individual situation.
ads.headline: Planned vs. Uncoordinated
ads.hook: There's a real difference between having a plan and having a coordinated one.
ads.infographic_direction:
Left column, "Coordinated plan":
- Trusted decision-makers named in advance
- Roth conversions timed with your tax picture
- Estate documents up to date and working together
- Heirs prepared and informed
- Charitable giving structured for income and legacy

Right column, "Without coordination":
- Court-appointed decision-makers
- Tax timing working against your distributions
- Outdated or conflicting estate documents
- Family left guessing
- Charitable intentions unfulfilled
ads.image_direction:
**Format:** 1080x1080 (square) | **Placement:** Feed
**Layout:** Split comparison (custom — not directly from archetypes)

```
COMPOSITION:
+--------------------------------------+
|  LOGO (top-center, white)            |
|                                      |
+------------------+-------------------+
|  COORDINATED     |  WITHOUT          |
|  (gold accent)   |  COORDINATION     |
|  (Rival Sans,    |  (white 60%)      |
|  label)          |  (Rival Sans)     |
|                  |                   |
|  [Dark navy      |  [Darker navy     |
|   #152d4b]       |   #0b1a2e]        |
|                  |                   |
|  ✓ Trusted       |  ✗ Court-appointed|
|    decision-     |    decision-      |
|    makers named  |    makers         |
|                  |                   |
|  ✓ Roth timed    |  ✗ Tax timing     |
|    with tax      |    works against  |
|    picture       |    you            |
|                  |                   |
|  ✓ Estate docs   |  ✗ Outdated or    |
|    up to date    |    conflicting    |
|                  |    documents      |
|  ✓ Heirs         |                   |
|    prepared      |  ✗ Family left    |
|                  |    guessing       |
|  ✓ Charitable    |                   |
|    giving        |  ✗ Intentions     |
|    structured    |    unfulfilled    |
|                  |                   |
+------------------+-------------------+
|                                      |
|  [SIGN UP] (outlined, white)         |
|  Wed, May 14 | Naples                |
+--------------------------------------+
```

**Design direction:** Two-column comparison on solid navy. Left column (coordinated) uses gold checkmarks (#c9a84c) and slightly lighter navy background. Right column (uncoordinated) uses white/red ✗ marks and darker navy. Thin vertical divider line (white, 1px, 30% opacity) separates columns.

**Diagonal line texture:** Lines-02.png at 5% opacity across the full background — adds brand texture to what would otherwise be a flat navy card.

**Typography:** Ivypresto for the column headers ("Coordinated Plan" / "Without Coordination"). Rival Sans Light for the bullet points. All white text.

**This is the one ad that breaks the photo-dominant pattern.** The infographic comparison IS the visual. Keep it clean, readable at phone size. Max 5 items per column.

ads.title: Expert insight video
ads.production: Brandon at desk or conference room. Conversational, not scripted. Lapel mic. Captions mandatory.
ads.primary_text:
Roth conversion timing is something people tend to set and forget. But the right year versus the wrong year can mean a significant difference in your tax bill — and for a lot of people in retirement, the window is narrowing.

It's one of several topics we'll explore together at our complimentary lunch on Wednesday, May 14 at The Capital Grille in Naples. Wealth manager Brandon Beck and estate planning attorney Ehren Frey.

When: Wednesday, May 14 at 12:00 PM
Where: The Capital Grille, Naples

Couples welcome.

RSVP here: [Landing Page Link]

*This presentation is for educational purposes only and is not legal, tax, or investment advice. The guest attorney is not affiliated with Confluence Financial Partners. Please consult your own advisors regarding your individual situation.

2. Middle: "The right year versus the wrong year can mean a significant difference in your tax bill. For a lot of people in retirement, the window is narrowing."
3. Bridge: "This is one of several things we'll cover at our complimentary lunch on May 14 at The Capital Grille. Couples welcome."
4. Close: "Details below. I think you'll be glad you came."

ads.image_direction:
**Format:** 1080x1920 (vertical) | **Placement:** Stories, Reels
**Layout:** Raw/authentic — NO designed template

```
STORIES/REELS:
+--------------------+
| [Progress bar]     |
|                    |
| [iPhone selfie     |
|  video: Brandon    |
|  holding phone,    |
|  natural light,    |
|  office or home    |
|  background.       |
|  NO graphics.      |
|  NO music.         |
|  NO brand overlay. |
|  Auto-generated    |
|  captions only.]   |
|                    |
| "This Wednesday    |
|  | A few seats     |
|  left"             |
| (Platform auto-    |
|  caption style,    |
|  NOT designed      |
|  overlay)          |
|                    |
| [SIGN UP]          |
| (platform CTA      |
|  sticker only)     |
+--------------------+
```

**Photo/video direction:** This ad intentionally looks NOTHING like the other 8. It's a raw iPhone selfie — Brandon picks up his phone, talks for 10-15 seconds, puts it down. Natural lighting. No branded elements visible (or incidental at most). No music. The only "design" is platform-generated captions.

**Why raw:** This is the authenticity play. After 3 weeks of polished branded ads, the pattern interrupt of a real person talking directly to you is the final conversion nudge. It should NOT look like an ad. If a viewer's first thought is "this is a real person talking to me," the creative is working.

**Do NOT add:** Logo overlay, brand colors, headline text, CTA button rendered into the image, music, or any post-production graphics. The platform CTA sticker is the only designed element.
